headline: Survey at 17% completion; strong regional concentration risks skewed insights.
collection_status: 226 of 1,350 responses collected (16.7% of target). LINDI dominates with 30% of all responses; 8 regions active but SINGIDA represents only 0.4% of submissions. Current pace suggests significant acceleration needed to meet 1,350 target.
key_findings: Lower primary teachers (83) and head teachers (49) comprise 60% of respondents; upper primary teachers (28), volunteers (25), and district-level officials (23 combined) underrepresented relative to programme footprint.
Programme expansion is clear priority: 'expand KF' (79 votes) and 'strengthen KF' (45 votes) total 124 responses (55% of top priorities), versus policy/new programmes at 97 combined.
Scale readiness score of 2.9/5 signals significant infrastructure/capacity gaps despite 4.5/5 partner satisfaction—disconnection between relationship quality and operational preparedness.

Feedback themes centre on sustained teacher incentives, government policy integration, and digital monitoring systems; limited mention of research or evidence suggests demand-supply mismatch on learning outcomes data.
flags: LINDI overrepresentation (30% of sample) and SINGIDA near-absence (0.4%) risk biasing findings toward one region's context; stratified sampling review urgent.
Scale readiness 2.9/5 paired with expansion priority suggests stakeholders want growth without confidence in systems to support it—potential implementation risk.
REO (2), ARC (5), and DEO (6) responses insufficient for district/regional-level strategy validation; deliberate outreach to administrative tier needed.
recommendation: Immediately audit sampling by region and respondent type against target distribution; redirect collection effort to undersampled regions (SINGIDA, SHINYANGA, SONGWE) and administrative officials. Simultaneously probe the 2.9 scale readiness score in qualitative follow-ups to identify specific bottlenecks before expansion commitments are locked in.
